mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k monthly additions signals robust labor market tightening, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher real returns to compensate for expected price pressures.
evidence_refs: fact:Current payroll level 139118k suggests ongoing but moderating job growth
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with tightening labor slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day windows following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero (0.09%) combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% creates negative real rates that compress Treasury yields downward as the Fed signals extended accommodation and market participants expect yield curve flattening.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.909 index
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of triggered 60-day windows, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1000k units signals weakening demand momentum and reduced inflation pressure, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as growth expectations contract and the Fed maintains accommodative policy longer.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1093k units suggests near-threshold weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 20-day windows following HOUST declines > 5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% monthly signals supply-side expansion and productivity gains that reduce unit labor costs, lowering inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 index shows modest production levels
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in < 45% of 5-day windows following months with INDPRO growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) declining simultaneously signals strong consumer demand and labor market tightness, increasing inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the Fed faces pressure to normalize rates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.4% AND UNRATE falls > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444377M and UNRATE at 6.1% show mixed signals
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term (RSAFS growth × UNRATE change) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced wage pressure, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as growth expectations decline and the Fed maintains or eases monetary accommodation.
regime_hint: labor market slack with downside growth risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 60-day windows following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL excluding food/energy proxy via headline index) accelerating above 2.0% YoY combined with payroll growth (PAYEMS) exceeding 150k monthly signals the Fed will begin rate normalization, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as market participants price in future tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.0% AND PAYEMS monthly change > 150k
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show moderate levels suggesting transition point
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL growth × PAYEMS level) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1000k signals late-cycle demand weakness, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as growth expectations contract and the Fed signals extended accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15994.3B and HOUST at 1093k suggest near-threshold conditions
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of triggered 60-day windows, or the interaction term (GDPC1 growth × HOUST level) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
